data.title: Lattice Acquires Mandala Technology, Advancing the New Way to Work with People + AI
data.description: On March 31, 2026, in San Francisco, Lattice revealed that Founder Tarun Galagali has joined the team to enhance the company's intelligent people platform. Lattice, known for fostering success between individuals and AI, confirmed the acquisition of Mandala's AI-native technology. This strategic move aims to bolster Lattice's capabilities in this field.

data.title: Resecurity Introduces Native Integration with Splunk
data.description: A cybersecurity and threat intelligence company, Resecurity (USA), known for working with Fortune 100 enterprises and government agencies, has recently unveiled a new integration with Splunk. Through a specially designed app available on Splunkbase, organizations can now easily link Resecurity’s cyber threat intelligence with the broader Splunk ecosystem. This integration allows for the swift intake of indicators of compromise, benefitting companies of all sizes.

data.title: FMI Expands Consulting Capabilities with Launch of Energy & Power Team and Addition of Senior Leaders
data.description: On March 31, 2026, in Raleigh, North Carolina, a prominent consulting and investment banking company, FMI Corporation, disclosed the incorporation of its consulting services into the energy and power industry. This expansion includes the recruitment of senior leaders like Evan. FMI Corporation is widely recognized for its services in the built environment and aims to bring its expertise to the energy and power sector. This strategic move is expected to significantly impact the company's operations and signifies its commitment to diversifying its consulting portfolio.

data.title: Series by Marriott Debuts in Japan with the Opening of Sugata Hotel Osaka Shinsaibashi
data.description: In Osaka, Japan, a new 256-room hotel has been launched, focusing on providing a unique blend of local charm and global connectivity for travelers. The hotel is designed to cater to curious individuals looking for authentic experiences. This property is part of the Series by Marriott brand and is included in Marriott Bonvoy's extensive collection of more than 30 exceptional hotels worldwide. The location aims to offer a distinctive hospitality experience to guests in the region, emphasizing a strong connection to the local culture while still maintaining international standards. The hotel is expected to attract a diverse range of guests seeking a balance between regional roots and global amenities.

data.title: Mercy Expands Primary Care Network with New Community Physician site in Westminster
data.description: Mercy Health Services has recently launched Mercy Personal Physicians at Westminster in Westminster, Maryland on March 31, 2026. This initiative is in line with their commitment to extending the 150-year legacy of the Sisters of Mercy in providing top-notch healthcare services. The establishment of this facility was commemorated through a dedicated ceremony, marking a significant milestone in the organization's mission to broaden access to quality healthcare.

data.title: Top Crunch Fitness franchisee CR Fitness launches Conquer Challenge member fitness competition
data.description: Red Bull leads the group of sponsors, which also includes Diamond Edge Construction, Matrix Fitness, Precor, PromoVault, The abs Company, Daxco, and Big Brand Graphics. In Tampa, Florida, on March 31, 2026, CR Fitness Holdings, the top Crunch Fitness franchisee and the fastest-growing one, announced this.

data.title: Bernard Looney to Become Chief Executive Officer of Prometheus Hyperscale
data.description: Bernard Looney will be taking on the roles of Chairman and Chief Executive Officer at Prometheus Hyperscale, a rapidly expanding data center developer. The company has disclosed plans to invest over $30 billion in hyperscale data center projects. This move comes as part of the company's strategy to push further into the high growth sector of data centers. The appointment of Bernard Looney, a former CEO of bp, aims to leverage his expertise in leading large-scale projects to drive the company's initiatives forward. The new leadership is expected to spearhead the development of cutting-edge data centers as part of Prometheus Hyperscale's aggressive expansion plans.

data.title: Gen Z Most Likely to Believe Revival Is Coming to America, New Data Shows
data.description: In Boulder, Colorado, recent research revealed by Barna Group and Gloo for the 2026 State of the Church initiative shows an increasing number of Americans expressing hope for a spiritual revival. Findings from a February 2026 survey indicate that almost 29% of U.S. adults are optimistic about the possibility of a spiritual revival, with Generation Z displaying the highest anticipation at 38%. Researchers highlight that this belief in potential spiritual transformation indicates a significant trend.

data.title: "New: NVIDIA CloudXR 6.0 offers 4K streaming to Apple Vision Pro with foveated rendering, and web access to Meta Quest 3."
data.description: CloudXR 6.0 from NVIDIA now allows for the streaming of 4K spatial computing content to Apple Vision Pro through foveated rendering. Additionally, it offers zero-install web access to Meta Quest 3.

data.title: Inc. Names Horatio No. 74 on Its 2026 List of the Fastest-Growing Private Companies in the Southeast
data.description: In Miami on March 31, 2026, the renowned media brand Inc. announced that Horatio, a cutting-edge outsourcing firm transforming customer experience and operations, has secured the 74th position on the 2026 Inc. list. This ranking showcases Horatio's significant impact and success in the entrepreneurial and business landscape.

data.title: Will Bitcoin's Four-Year Cycle Persist As Institutional Influence Grows?
data.description: The recent stagnant movement of Bitcoin (CRYPTO: BTC) within a narrow trading range is sparking discussions about the continuation or potential fading of its traditional four-year cycle, with the debate intensifying in a recent post by Arkham Intelligence on March 31. The analytics platform pointed out the increasing institutional participation in the market, suggesting that this could be altering Bitcoin's market dynamics. Institutional investors tend to invest in a more organized manner, following set schedules and risk management strategies, which could lead to decreased volatility and a smoothing out of the typical boom-and-bust cycles seen in Bitcoin's history. This shift is attributed to factors like increased institutional involvement and potential impacts of macroeconomic conditions on the market. For further details, the complete article is accessible on Benzinga.com.
